title: The Creature
excerpt: Coming-of-age fiction following Jay — physics projects before dawn, the long route past an old playground, and a recurring caramel nightmare he can't shake.
content: Jay was playing in pools filled with caramel, surrounded by cities made of chocolate and marshmallows. Diving into his pool he slurps up as much caramel as he can fill into his mouth. He slowly swallows all of it, as he begins to sink deeper and deeper in the confectionary filled pool. He continues to sink, eating candy after candy, only consuming more as he descends. Through the endless abyss of sugary delight, floating glittery unicorns made of marshmallows, and many hundreds of chocolate bunnies all floating along with him. As he keeps on consuming these sweets, his eyes grow heavy, and he struggles to see in the caramel around him. He was able to breathe somehow, which came to his attention rather late, as he found that nothing made any sense. As he keeps sinking, his chest tightens as the pressure of the caramel begins to weigh down upon him. He coughs, and gasps for air as his lungs are filled with heavy syrup. His eyes jolt open as he begins to struggle and flail around, but his efforts were to no avail as he continued to sink, until he hit the bottom, where he promptly woke up from this interesting dream of his. He sat upright in his bed, looking around the room wildly.

title: Continuity
excerpt: Book 2 of the 4Corner Collection. A grounded, funny high-school story where a kid accidentally activates a hidden power — and misunderstands exactly how it works.
content: Chocolate appeared on my desk again. No note, no warning — just there, between my notebook and the edge where the wood is worn smooth. Across the room my best friend was already asleep, the way he always is by second period, right up until a marker bounced off his forehead. Coding class. We were supposed to be writing loops. I was playing Minecraft. That's the part they caught me for — not the other thing, the thing I'd done by accident that morning that I still didn't understand. By lunch we were doing the McDonald's run like nothing was different. By the end of the day I was sitting in the principal's office, and the chocolate was still in my pocket.

title: Loss of Power
excerpt: Book 3 of the 4Corner Collection. Roughly 800 years from now, screens plug straight into your eyes — and a quiet collector keeps chasing the old analog things the world threw away.
content: I almost made it to my dorm door before the light found me. The coffee machine — antique, heavy, beautiful in the way only useless old things are — was gone from my hands before I understood the light was a confiscation and not just a glare. I went back to my bunk and powered on my eyeset out of habit. News I didn't want, scores I didn't care about, knowledge I hadn't asked for, painted across the inside of my eyes where no one else could see it. I watched it for a while. Then I sighed, switched it off, and looked at the rest of my collection: shelves of devices, limited editions and vintage models, all of them silent, none of them the thing I actually wanted.

title: Untitled (with Nathan)
excerpt: Book 4 of the 4Corner Collection, co-written with Nathan. A psychological thriller where Bob's dreams are haunted by a demon, and the line between a facility and a dream keeps blurring.
content: He barricaded the door with the cabinet and gripped the bat he'd made himself — nails driven through the head, never once used. On the other side his brother's voice begged him to open it. He didn't. It wasn't his brother; he was sure of that, as sure as he'd ever been of anything, and being sure was the only thing keeping his hands from the handle. The door opened on its own.

title: A Breakup Letter to 2025
excerpt: A short experimental piece — a breakup letter addressed to a year, written in the shape of famous correspondence.
content: Dear 2025, we both knew it wasn't going to last twelve months. You were thrilling and exhausting in equal measure...
